message:
feat(runtime): wire trauma guard config through runtime and pane registry with hot reload

Connect TraumaGuardConfig to the runtime pipeline so per-pane trauma
state is initialized from config and can be hot-reloaded without restart.

Runtime changes (runtime.rs):
- RuntimeConfig gains trauma_guard: TraumaGuardConfig field
- ObservationRuntime::new() passes trauma guard config to
  PaneRegistry::with_filter_and_trauma() at construction time
- HotReloadableConfig includes trauma_guard for live reload
- Discovery task hot-reload loop detects trauma guard config changes
  and calls reg.set_trauma_guard_config() to propagate new thresholds
  to all existing panes
- Fix stale SPSC references in relay task doc comments (code was
  already SPMC from prior commit, comments lagged behind)
- New test: runtime_spmc_handoff_preserves_exact_output_checksum —
  injects 48 burst chunks into a mock pane, verifies persisted
  segments are contiguous, content matches via SHA-256 isomorphism
  proof, and no ingest gaps exist

Pane registry changes (ingest.rs):
- PaneRegistry gains trauma_guard_config: TraumaGuardConfig field
- New constructor with_filter_and_trauma() accepts both filter and
  trauma config at initialization
- set_trauma_guard_config(): on config change, reinitializes all
  per-pane TraumaState instances with updated thresholds
- discovery_tick uses stored config when registering new panes
- record_trauma_command_result() short-circuits when
  trauma_guard_config.enabled == false (returns allow, no intervention)
- When enabled, uses trauma_guard_config.to_trauma_config() for lazy
  initialization of per-pane trauma state
- 2 new tests: disabled guard skips intervention, config reload
  resets thresholds on existing panes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
